title: Take out 'personally I thinks' — public-speaking self-feedback
html:
TL;DR

  • 17yo Yahir critiques his own public speaking in real-time: "take out 'personally I thinks'... keep it natural... don't get caught up on stats... work on tone... don't talk just to talk" — likely an FBLA / Al Forthan presentation prep
  • Mixes critique with self-affirmation in the same note: "great transitions, good distribution of talking time, I liked my energy, don't be too official" — the Yahir self-coaching pattern in early form
  • The "don't talk just to talk" line previews the much-later Vittorio aphorism cluster and asking the right question discipline — verbal economy as a 2021 problem he's already naming
  • Single-page mid-presentation Apple Note as the speech-coach is myself artifact — no external mentor invoked
  • Earliest explicit public speaking self-improvement note in the corpus; the Al Forthan prep was likely the immediate context (Jan 2021)

Notes

The interest of this note is the structure: two columns of self-feedback merged into one stream — the criticisms ("take out 'personally I thinks'") sit a few lines above the praise ("I liked my energy"). It's a self-rubric, the kind a debate-coach would write, except Yahir is filling both roles. He is his own first speech coach, and the date (mid-Jan 2021) places this likely in the run-up to the Al Forthan memorial scholarship oration (2021 01 22 al forthan charlee stats question from five days later).

"Don't talk just to talk" reads, in retrospect, as the seed of every later Yahir-aphorism-about-verbal-discipline: the Mr. Phillips substitute era "compound your shit" lessons get internalized as adult-Yahir's "verbal economy" by 2024. This note is one of the earliest waypoints on that arc.

The "Timer" mid-page is presumably a stopwatch reference — practice timing his own talks. The structure ("Timer" centered, then a blank line, then more notes) suggests this was annotated during/after a recorded practice run.

Connections

public speaking · fbla · al forthan · 2021 01 22 al forthan charlee stats question · asking the right question · grants pass teen era · self coaching pattern
